title: dactylorhiza incarnata var. obscura
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. obscura, commonly known as the dark-flowered marsh orchid, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Dactylorhiza incarnata species. This plant is known for its unique dark purple flowers, which are less common than the typical pink or white varieties found in the same species. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata var. obscura include its potential in treating cardiovascular issues, such as high blood pressure and varicose veins, due to its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic actions, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects.

parts: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. obscura has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, and flowers. The roots of Dactylorhiza incarnata var. obscura contain glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves and flowers of the plant are rich in flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. The rhizomes of Dactylorhiza incarnata var. obscura also contain cardenolides, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and cardiac-stimulating effects.
side_effects: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. obscura can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high saponin content. Additionally, excessive consumption may lead to headaches, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. There is also concern that long-term use of this plant may cause kidney damage and interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. If not prepared correctly, Dactylorhiza incarnata var. obscura may also cause skin irritation and allergic contact dermatitis.
